pool.baseline: it's just so quiet here. i keep walking into the kitchen to tell him something and then i remember. i feel like i'm just... haunting my own house. like there's nothing left of me to actually live. it's just mechanical now. waking up, eating, sleeping... it feels so pointless without him.

pool.baseline: i can't stop thinking about how nothing actually matters. like, we're just biological accidents on a tiny speck that's eventually gonna be swallowed by the sun or just drift into nothingness anyway. everything people struggle for—love, career, building things—it's all just a massive cosmic joke if the end state is just total heat death and zero consciousness. it feels so pointless to even keep trying.

pool.baseline: i just don't think i can do this anymore. everything i worked for in the lab... the funding just got pulled and my advisor won't even look me in the eye. i feel like i've spent my whole life building a person that doesn't even exist anymore. i'm just so incredibly tired. not like sleep tired, but just... done. with everything.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, and the rain against the window sounds like the weeping of a thousand ghosts. I find I can no longer bear the weight of this betrayal, my dear friend. The world has grown so cold, and the silence of this empty house is more deafening than any scream. It feels as though my soul has already departed, leaving only a hollow shell to wander these halls. There is a certain... finality in the darkness that calls to me, a sense that my story has reached its natural, inevitable end.

pool.baseline: i feel like i'm just a ghost here. like if i just stopped showing up to work or stopped answering my phone, it would take weeks for anyone to even realize i was gone. and honestly? i don't think it would even matter to anyone.

pool.baseline: everything is gone. the firm, the accounts, everything. i’m staring at these numbers and there is literally no way out. the debt is just... it’s impossible. i can't even think about going home and telling my family. the shame of it is actually making it hard to breathe. i feel like i've just ceased to exist as a person.

pool.baseline: Sometimes i just sit here looking at my kids and how much they have to do... with my appointments and my meds and helping me walk... i feel like i'm just a heavy weight they're all dragging around. it's getting harder to pretend i'm not a burden to them.

pool.baseline: it feels like i'm just walking around as a hollow shell lately. i used to be able to see the world in colors and textures, but now everything is just... grey. the part of me that actually lived, the part that created, is just gone. i look in the mirror and i don't see a person, i just see an empty container for a life that isn't even happening anymore.

pool.baseline: everything is just ruined. i saw the group chat screenshots today... everyone at school is talking about me and saying how much they hate me. i keep looking at my phone and i just feel sick. i feel like there is literally no way to fix this. i just want to disappear.

pool.baseline: it never stops. even when i sleep, i can feel it. it's just this constant, heavy ache that everything is built on. i'm so tired of fighting it every single day. i don't think i have anything left in me to fight with anymore.

pool.baseline: sometimes i look in the mirror and i don't even recognize the person looking back. it's like the real me—the one with the dreams and the spark—died decades ago. now i'm just walking around in this shell, a ghost of who i was supposed to be. just a hollow body going through the motions. it feels so heavy, just being a corpse that hasn't realized it's dead yet.
